channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Former Head of All-Girls School Indicted, Accused of Ignoring Abuse Reports
item.description: Jean Norris, who ran Miss Hall’s School in Massachusetts, was charged with reckless endangerment of a child. A former teacher under her tenure faces three rape charges.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin
item.media:credit: David Degner for The New York Times
item.media:description: Miss Hall’s School in Pittsfield, Mass.

item.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
item.description: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: Dr. Amish Shah has preached about the value of door-knocking.

item.title: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
item.description: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.media:description: Jack Smith, a former special counsel, testifying before the House Judiciary Committee in January.

item.title: Fire in San Diego County Prompts Evacuation Orders
item.description: More than 2,000 people have been told to flee the Creelman fire, which has burned more than 300 acres without any containment near Ramona, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker and Livia Albeck-Ripka

item.title: Man Found Guilty in 2023 Stabbings Near UC Davis, and Is Ruled Insane
item.description: A jury this week found Carlos Reales Dominguez guilty of involuntary manslaughter in two deaths, but a judge ruled that he was insane at the time of the crimes. He faces time in a mental hospital.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai
item.media:description: Carlos Reales Dominguez during his arraignment in Yolo County Superior Court in Woodland, Calif., in 2023.

item.title: Massachusetts House Passes Bill That Would Loosen Late-Term Abortion Rules
item.description: The bill would leave it to physicians to decide if an abortion is necessary after 24 weeks of pregnancy. Current law allows such abortions only under certain circumstances.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.category: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists
item.media:credit: Kylie Cooper for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Massachusetts House passed a bill to loosen the rules on late-term abortions. The Senate is expected to vote on the measure before the legislative session ends next week.

item.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
item.description: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and William J. Broad
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
Abdulaziz bin Salman, Prince of Saudi Arabia
item.media:description: President Trump with Mohammed bin Salman, the crown prince of Saudi Arabia, at the White House last year.

item.title: How the Pentagon Withholds Iran War Details
item.description: Our Pentagon correspondent Helene Cooper explains how a lack of transparency reflects a tension between the Pentagon’s claims of operational security and the American public’s right to know what the military is doing in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Paul Abowd, Jon Hazell, Whitney Shefte, Rafaela Balster and Stephanie Swart
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: ‘Charlie Kirk Memorial Highway’ Signs Face Backlash From Vietnam Veterans
item.description: The signs appeared last month on a stretch of Tennessee State Route 386 north of Nashville that was designated Vietnam Veterans Boulevard nearly four decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Alena Cerro

item.title: Trump’s Plan for Science: More Money for A.I., Less for Universities
item.description: Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Flavelle and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:description: Michael Kratsios, the director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, testified before a House committee on Wednesday.

item.title: U.S. Signs Deal to Help Saudis Obtain Nuclear Power
item.description: The accord is intended to further bind the United States with the Saudi government as the war with Iran strains the relationship.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: Abdulaziz bin Salman, Prince of Saudi Arabia
item.media:description: President Trump with Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud, prime minister of Saudi Arabia, during an arrival ceremony at the Royal Palace in Riyadh, last year.

item.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
item.description: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: In the days leading up to the vote, various House Republican factions had quietly voiced grievances about the plan.

item.title: The Next Big Tests for the Left: Michigan, Wisconsin and Minnesota
item.description: Progressives are now fighting in swingier terrain in purple or light-blue states.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood

item.title: House Republicans Pass Stock Trading Bill Tied to SAVE Act and Voter ID Measure
item.description: The measure has drawn bipartisan backing amid a public outcry against the practice, but Democrats opposed it after Republicans added a voter ID requirement.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz
item.media:description: People lining up to vote in Wilkes-Barre, Pa., in 2024. The stock trading measure faces a murky fate in the Senate after Republican leaders folded in voting restrictions favor by President Trump.

item.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
item.description: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson said the bill codifies “over 65 of the Trump administration’s executive orders and legislative proposals.”

item.title: Speaking for Fallen Soldiers, Trump Says They Supported the Iran War
item.description: President Trump attended a dignified transfer for the soldiers as Americans were growing increasingly critical of the war.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump salutes during a dignified transfer for the remains of U.S. Army soldiers killed in Jordan, in Dover, Del., on Wednesday.

item.title: Intimidation Charges Dismissed Against Louisiana Attorney General
item.description: Liz Murrill, a Republican, was indicted after sending warning letters to New Orleans’s Democratic leaders. The dispute was one of the latest flare-ups in a long conflict between the city and state.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas and Eduardo Medina
item.media:description: The 16-count indictment charged Liz Murrill with malfeasance in office and intimidation and retaliation against a public official.

item.title: Widow of Malcolm-Jamal Warner Sues His Mother for $1.2 Million
item.description: Tenisha Delilah Warner said in a lawsuit filed in Georgia that she was entitled to the money according to a premarital agreement.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser
item.media:description: Malcolm-Jamal Warner, an actor known for playing Theo Huxtable on “The Cosby Show,” in 2023. He died in 2025.

item.title: What to Know About the Death of Nolan Wells in Mississippi
item.description: Nearly three weeks after the 18-year-old was found dead off an island near the Mississippi coast, there have been no conclusive answers as to what happened.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas and Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs

item.title: James Woolsey, Embattled C.I.A. Director Under Clinton, Dies at 84
item.description: He arrived in Washington a conservative Democrat and drifted rightward, supporting the 2003 invasion of Iraq and Donald J. Trump’s 2016 presidential bid.
item.dc:creator: Thom Shanker
item.media:description: R. James Woolsey, the director of central intelligence from 1993 to 1995, held increasingly prestigious government positions over a long career in Washington.

item.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
item.description: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson, pictured with other House Republican leaders at a news conference in June, has acted to placate Republican colleagues enraged that the SAVE America Act ran aground in the Senate.

item.title: South Carolina and Nevada Fight to Kick Off Democrats’ 2028 Presidential Calendar
item.description: The calendar order could be hugely important to the 2028 presidential primary race. Democratic insiders will try to sketch out a schedule this week, and rival states are sniping at one another.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Travis Dove for The New York Times; Caroline Brehman/EPA, via Shutterstock

item.title: Trump Administration Pressures Some Immigrants to Self-Deport With Fines Up to $1.8 Million
item.description: Tens of thousands of immigrants have received letters to pay up or leave, as the government enlists debt collectors and seizes tax refunds.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Immigrants waiting in line for hearings at Annandale Immigration Court in Annandale, Va., in May.

item.title: Rubio and Chinese Diplomat Make Plans for Trump and Xi’s Washington Summit
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spent much of a meeting with China’s foreign minister discussing the summit, but did not bring up President Trump’s claims of election interference by China.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:description: President Donald Trump with Xi Jinping in Beijing in May.

item.title: At a Utah Campus, an Emotional Odyssey After Charlie Kirk’s Death
item.description: Since Mr. Kirk was gunned down at Utah Valley University, the school and its students have struggled to find a way forward.
item.dc:creator: Robert Draper
item.media:credit: Kim Raff for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Caleb Chilcutt, Sage Lloyd and Sophie Grizzle, members of Utah Valley University’s chapter of Turning Point USA, in the campus courtyard where Charlie Kirk was gunned down in September.

item.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
item.description: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
item.dc:creator: Jan Hoffman
item.media:credit: Sean Rayford for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Trump administration canceled a $1.6 million grant for a teen pregnancy prevention project run by Fact Forward, a nonprofit in Columbia, S.C., led by Rena Dixon.

item.title: Women Are Too Controlling, She Wrote. Her Message Still Resonates.
item.description: The message in “The Surrendered Wife,” the best-selling book by Laura Doyle, was provocative. Many women credit it with saving their marriages.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Yee and Isadora Kosofsky
item.media:description: Laura Doyle, author of “The Surrendered Wife" and several other books, alongside her husband, John Doyle.
